name: TRM Blockchain Intelligence
what: Full transaction graph, wallet balances, risk scores, entity attribution, chain hops across 40+ chains including BTC, ETH, TRON, SOL, XMR
buyerValue: Prosecutors need chain-of-custody wallet tracing. FinCEN SAR writers need risk scores. Exchanges need real-time screening.
keyQuestion: Can you trace through mixers and chain hops? — Yes. TRM traces through Tornado Cash, JoinMarket, XMR bridges.

name: Chainabuse Victim Reports
what: Victim-reported crypto scam and fraud addresses. 500K+ reports. API and Pro access with full metadata.
returns: Scam category (rug pull, romance, ransomware, pig butchering)
buyerValue: LE: mass victim triage. Exchanges: block reported deposit addresses before funds are mixed. FinServ: SAR-ready victim data.
keyQuestion: Is this a full address list or just summaries? — Full metadata including associated infrastructure (domains, social handles, amounts).

name: Flashpoint Intelligence
what: Dark web marketplace data, threat actor profiles, ransomware victim listings, compromised credentials, forum activity across 1,000+ monitored communities.
returns: Forum post with username, timestamp, content
Threat actor profile with aliases and TTPs
Ransomware victim listing with negotiation details
sample: {"type":"forum_post","actor":"LOCKBIT_LEADER","forum":"RAMP","content":"New victim: MegaCorp Inc. 500GB exfiltrated. Ransom: $2.1M. Deadline: 72hrs.","timestamp":"2026-04-21T14:32:00Z"}
buyerValue: John Chirhart note: "Customers ask about Flashpoint depth." Full post content, not just headlines. Actor attribution.
keyQuestion: Do we get full post content or just metadata? — Full content including unstructured forum text, actor handles, and infrastructure indicators.

name: Webz.io Dark Web Monitor
what: Real-time crawl of dark web forums, paste sites, Telegram channels, and open web leak sites. 10M+ new posts/month.
returns: Entity mentions extracted (companies, people, wallets, IPs)
Ransomware countdown timers and victim data
buyerValue: SEC disclosure buyers: "Is our name being discussed on dark web forums right now?" Real-time answer.
keyQuestion: Is this raw scrape or structured data? — Structured JSON with entity extraction. Raw text also available for Orion NLQ.

name: Pipl People Intelligence
what: People data from 3B+ profiles across social networks, professional directories, public records, and leaked databases. Identity resolution across aliases.
buyerValue: LE: link crypto wallet operator to real-world identity. FinServ: KYC on beneficial owners. Critical for DPRK/OFAC attribution.
keyQuestion: Do we get full email addresses or just domains? — Full email addresses where available in commercial data. Some jurisdictions have privacy restrictions.

name: Censys Attack Surface Intelligence
what: Internet-wide scanning covering 5B+ devices. Certificates, open ports, services, hosting relationships, and infrastructure clustering.
buyerValue: Ransomware attribution: "Find all hosts sharing infrastructure with this C2 IP." Scam investigation: expand one domain to full infrastructure.
keyQuestion: How far can it expand? — Unlimited hops via shared certificate, IP, ASN. Orion automates the expansion.

name: SpyCloud Breach Intelligence
what: Recovered breach data from criminal underground: credentials, PII, payment data from 700+ breach events. Enterprise and consumer exposure.
returns: Associated username and plaintext password where available
buyerValue: THIS IS THE "FULL EMAIL OR JUST HEADERS" QUESTION — SpyCloud returns full plaintext credentials where recovered from underground. Not just headers.
keyQuestion: Do we get the full credentials or just exposure notification? — FULL plaintext credentials where available in the underground data. This is specifically what SEC/FBI investigators need for attribution.

name: Exa Web Search & Intelligence
what: Neural web search optimised for intelligence gathering. Finds relevant pages, extracts entities, and surfaces open-web intelligence that standard search misses.
returns: Relevant page URLs with content extracts
Full page text for NLQ processing
sample: {"query":"Lazarus Group BTC wallets 2026","results":[{"url":"https://cisa.gov/...","extract":"...Lazarus Group has been linked to wallets...","entities":["1FzW...","bc1q..."]}]}
buyerValue: Open-source starting point for any investigation. Orion uses Exa to build initial intelligence before pivoting to paid data sources.
keyQuestion: Is this just web search? — Neural search with entity extraction and full page text. Better signal-to-noise than Google for intelligence tasks.

name: Firecrawl Targeted Web Scrape
what: On-demand crawl of specific URLs, forums, marketplaces, and leak sites. Returns structured content with entity extraction.
returns: Full page content as structured JSON
Tables, lists, and key data extracted
buyerValue: LE: scrape a dark web marketplace vendor page and extract all listings. Automates what analysts used to do manually over Tor.
keyQuestion: Does this work on .onion sites? — Yes, Orion handles Tor routing for .onion crawls via Firecrawl integration.

name: OpenCorporates Business Registry
what: 130M+ company records from 140+ national registries. Company ownership, directors, incorporation history, registered addresses.
sample: {"company":"Rapid Crypto LLC","reg_number":"L23000123456","jurisdiction":"FL_US","incorporated":"2023-01-14","directors":["Ivan Petrov","Elena Smirnova"],"address":"1234 SW 8th St, Miami FL"}
buyerValue: DPRK money laundering: trace shell companies in FL/WY to real beneficial owners. Scam investigation: find who registered the scam company.
keyQuestion: Is this just US companies? — 140+ jurisdictions including offshore (BVI, Cayman, Seychelles). Critical for sanctions evasion investigations.

name: TRM Entity Graph
what: Entity-level view linking multiple addresses to named services, exchanges, mixing services, and sanctioned entities. Updated daily from LE intelligence.
returns: Risk flags (OFAC sanctioned, darknet market, mixer)
buyerValue: Exchanges: "Is this deposit address associated with a sanctioned entity?" Instant answer with entity-level context.
keyQuestion: How current is the entity data? — Updated within 24 hours of new LE intelligence. OFAC designations reflected same day.

name: Direct Blockchain Node Queries
what: Direct node access for BTC, ETH, TRON, and 20+ additional chains. Mempool monitoring, raw transaction data, real-time event streaming.
returns: Smart contract events (ERC-20 transfers, DEX swaps)
Real-time webhook alerts on watched addresses
buyerValue: Exchanges: real-time monitoring of deposits before confirmation. LE: detect and interdict in-flight transactions before they confirm.
keyQuestion: Can we monitor addresses in real-time? — Yes. Webhook alerts for watched address activity within seconds of mempool entry.

name: TRM Transaction Monitoring
what: Real-time screening of incoming/outgoing transactions against sanctions lists, darknet markets, ransomware wallets, and high-risk services. Sub-second response.
returns: Matched entity name and sanction list
buyerValue: Exchanges: screen every deposit/withdrawal in real time. FinServ: SAR-ready evidence with entity attribution for FinCEN reporting.
keyQuestion: How fast is it? — Sub-200ms average. No impact on transaction throughput.

name: TRM Wallet Screening API
what: Batch wallet screening against TRM entity database. Accepts CSV upload of up to 100K addresses. Returns risk profiles, entity labels, and exposure scores.
buyerValue: AML batch processing. Exchanges can screen entire customer address books overnight. FinServ can validate counterparty wallets before correspondent banking.
keyQuestion: Does it cover all chains? — 40+ chains including BTC, ETH, TRON, SOL, XMR (limited), BSC, Polygon, and all major EVM chains.

name: TRM Sanctions Screening
what: Crypto-specific sanctions screening against OFAC SDN, EU Consolidated List, UN Security Council, FATF grey/black lists, and 40+ national sanctions regimes.
returns: Sanctioned entity name and designation date
sample: {"address":"bc1q...","ofac_match":true,"entity":"Tornado Cash (OFAC SDN, 08/2022)","designation_legal_basis":"IEEPA","associated_addresses":234,"guidance":"Prohibited for US persons per OFAC FAQs 1076-1078"}
buyerValue: Travel Rule compliance. Exchanges need this for VASP-to-VASP transfers. FinServ needs it for crypto custody and prime brokerage services.
keyQuestion: How quickly are new OFAC designations reflected? — Within 2 hours of OFAC publication.

name: Recorded Future Intelligence
what: Structured threat intelligence from 1.5M+ sources. Threat actor profiles, CVE intelligence, malware analysis, geopolitical risk signals, and dark web monitoring.
returns: Threat actor profile with TTP matrix
CVE risk score and exploitation likelihood
buyerValue: SEC buyers: "What threat actors are targeting our sector?" Orion fuses RF threat intel with TRM blockchain tracing for end-to-end attribution.
keyQuestion: How is this different from standalone RF? — Orion uses RF as a data source within AI-native investigation workflows. Natural language queries across RF + blockchain simultaneously.

name: ZeroFOX Digital Risk & DRPS
what: Social media and surface/dark web monitoring for brand threats, executive impersonation, credential leaks, and phishing infrastructure targeting your organisation.
returns: Dark web data sale listings mentioning your organisation
buyerValue: Financial institutions: "Is anyone selling our employee credentials right now?" Pre-breach detection before threat actors use the access.
keyQuestion: Is this real-time? — Continuous monitoring with alert latency typically under 4 hours for dark web forum posts.

name: IntSights / Rapid7 Threat Intel
what: Closed-source threat intelligence from criminal underground. HUMINT-enriched threat actor profiles, fraud scheme detection, and financial crime intelligence.
returns: Threat actor profile with aliases and infrastructure
Underground forum post with full content
sample: {"actor":"EVIL_CORP","forum":"exploit.in","post_content":"New DanaBot campaign targeting US banks this week...","targeted_banks":["Chase","BofA"],"iocs":["45.142.x.x","danabot-c2.net"]}
buyerValue: Financial institutions: "Are we being specifically targeted by a threat actor?" Pre-attack intelligence, not post-incident response.
keyQuestion: What makes this different from Flashpoint? — IntSights has stronger HUMINT sourcing for financial crime specifically; Flashpoint broader dark web coverage.

name: LexisNexis Risk Solutions
what: Identity verification and fraud detection combining public records, credit header data, utility records, and cross-industry fraud intelligence from 10,000+ contributors.
returns: Identity score with fraud risk indicators
Phone number ownership and risk score
buyerValue: KYC/AML onboarding. Exchanges need identity verification at account opening. Banks need it for SAR investigations to confirm beneficial owners.
keyQuestion: Is this real-name data or just statistical profiles? — Real identity records from authoritative US public records and credit bureau headers. Not available in all jurisdictions.

name: Truecaller Phone Intelligence
what: Phone number intelligence covering 3B+ numbers globally. Caller ID data, spam scores, name lookup, and telecom metadata for APAC and emerging markets.
returns: Social media accounts linked to number
buyerValue: Critical for APAC investigations where Pipl coverage is weaker. Singapore, Indonesia, India phone attribution that Western databases miss.
keyQuestion: Is this available globally? — Strong in APAC, South Asia, Africa, Middle East. Weaker in EU/US where GDPR limits data.

name: Dow Jones Risk & Compliance
what: PEP screening, adverse media, and sanctions screening across 5M+ entities. Financial crime risk assessment for onboarding and ongoing monitoring.
returns: PEP status and political exposure level
Adverse media score with article excerpts
sample: {"entity":"Alexander Ivanov","pep_status":true,"pep_category":"Government Official - Russia","sanctions_matches":["EU Consolidated List"],"adverse_media_score":8.7,"articles":["FT: Oligarch linked to sanctioned bank..."]}
buyerValue: Crypto exchange KYC: mandatory PEP screening for Travel Rule compliance. Banks: Enhanced Due Diligence on high-risk customer onboarding.
keyQuestion: Is this just for onboarding or ongoing monitoring too? — Both. Orion can trigger re-screening when new adverse media or sanctions are detected.

name: Refinitiv World-Check
what: Global database of PEPs, sanctioned individuals, and entities with enhanced due diligence profiles. 5.5M+ records with structured risk assessments.
returns: Profile with risk category and sub-category
sample: {"profile":"Mukhtar Ablyazov","category":"PEP/Sanctions","jurisdictions":["EU","UK","UA"],"associates":["BTA Bank","Ilyas Khrapunov"],"confidence":0.97,"sources":["Interpol Red Notice","UK Crown Court judgment"]}
buyerValue: Global standard for correspondent banking due diligence. Orion uses World-Check as structured input into AI investigation workflows.
keyQuestion: How does this differ from Dow Jones? — World-Check stronger on international/EMEA coverage; Dow Jones stronger adverse media. Orion uses both.

name: Shodan Internet Intelligence
what: Internet-connected device index covering 1.5B+ services. IoT discovery, vulnerability exposure, industrial control system identification, and network mapping.
buyerValue: Ransomware attribution: "What other devices share this threat actor infrastructure?" Map C2 servers via shared certificate and hosting patterns.
keyQuestion: Is this passive scanning or active? — Passive index updated continuously. No active scanning of customer environments. Safe for LE operations.

name: VirusTotal Malware Intelligence
what: Malware analysis platform with 70+ AV engine verdicts, file/URL/IP/domain reputation, and behavioural sandbox analysis from 2M+ daily submissions.
buyerValue: Incident response: "Is this file malicious and who wrote it?" Ransomware attribution from malware sample back to threat actor group.
keyQuestion: Does Orion get access to sandbox reports? — Yes. Full dynamic analysis reports including network connections, registry changes, and process trees.

name: GreyNoise Internet Noise Analysis
what: Classifies background internet noise vs. targeted attacks. Identifies mass scanning activity, separates it from real threats, and enriches IPs with attacker behaviour context.
returns: Attack type and target (mass scan vs. targeted)
buyerValue: SOC teams: "Is this IP scanning everyone or just us?" Targeted attack = high priority. Mass scan = low priority. Orion uses this for alert triage.
keyQuestion: How current is the data? — Real-time. GreyNoise observes 1M+ IPs/day across 9K+ honeypot sensors globally.

name: Have I Been Pwned Enterprise
what: Notification and lookup service for 14B+ pwned credentials across 700+ breaches. Enterprise API for domain-wide monitoring and bulk lookup.
returns: Compromised data types (email, password, phone, DOB)
buyerValue: Proactive corporate exposure monitoring. "How many of our employees have credentials exposed in recent breaches?" Pre-credential-stuffing defence.
keyQuestion: Is this the same as SpyCloud? — HIBP is broader breach coverage but fewer raw credentials. SpyCloud has more plaintext passwords from criminal underground. Orion uses both.

name: DeHashed Credential Intelligence
what: Aggregated breach database covering 15B+ records from hundreds of breach events. Lookup by email, username, phone, IP, or domain. Criminal underground sourcing.
returns: All breaches containing the queried value
Plaintext or hashed passwords where available
IP addresses and phone numbers from same breach
buyerValue: Investigation: trace a target across multiple breaches to build a comprehensive credential profile. LE attribution: link underground persona to real identity via email.
keyQuestion: Are passwords full plaintext or hashed? — Mixed. Plaintext where available in criminal data. SHA/MD5/bcrypt hashes otherwise. Orion flags which format.

name: ComplyAdvantage AML Data
what: AI-generated AML data covering sanctions, PEPs, adverse media, and fitness-and-probity checks. Real-time updates from 30,000+ global sources.
returns: Adverse media with full article text
buyerValue: VASP onboarding: screen new business customers against AML data before allowing trading. Travel Rule counterparty due diligence.
keyQuestion: How often is the data refreshed? — Continuous for sanctions (minutes). Adverse media scraped every 4 hours. PEP status reviewed quarterly.

name: Elliptic Compliance Data
what: Blockchain analytics focused on compliance screening for financial institutions. Strong in EDD workflows and regulatory reporting. Limited investigation depth vs. Orion.
returns: Wallet risk score with compliance classification
buyerValue: Competitive positioning: Elliptic is compliance-only. Orion has Elliptic-equivalent compliance screening PLUS deep investigation, dark web fusion, and NLQ. Show the gap.
keyQuestion: Does Orion replace Elliptic? — For investigation yes. For pure compliance workflows, position as superior. Many customers run both until POC proves Orion covers both.
